title: Cross-run synthesis: HLA-A expression-biophysics correlations replicate at |rho| ~0.7 across two premise sets (exp24 n=69 and exp26 n=19/HLA-A subset)

subject.display: Cross-run synthesis: HLA-A expression-biophysics correlations replicate at |rho| ~0.7 across two premise sets (exp24 n=69 and exp26 n=19/HLA-A subset)

parts.prose: Constituent claim hla_a_dg37_vs_tpm of the composite synthesis: HLA-A expression prediction is robust across two independent premise sets (ΔG37 + CpG density), supporting the joint coupling hypothesis.

Constituent claim hla_a_cpg_density_vs_tpm of the composite synthesis: HLA-A expression prediction is robust across two independent premise sets (ΔG37 + CpG density), supporting the joint coupling hypothesis.

source.note: Per-locus rho summary from the exp24 provenance run (n=69 alleles across 6 loci, Bettens 2022).
Per-locus rho summary from the exp26 provenance run (n=22,259 alleles total; HLA-A subset n=19 protein-distinct alleles with measured TPM).

statistics.name: spearman_rho_per_run_dg37_vs_tpm
statistics.note: Per-run dG37 vs TPM Spearman rho. Two independent premise sets (different cohorts, different probe selection rules) produce indistinguishable point estimates.

statistics.name: spearman_rho_per_run_cpg_vs_tpm
statistics.note: Per-run CpG density vs TPM Spearman rho. Replication holds for the opposite-signed companion.

statistics.name: max_abs_diff_rho_across_runs_dg37
statistics.note: Derived: max absolute difference between any two per-run rhos for dG37. Tiny (~0.001) -> point estimates agree to 3 decimal places.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts: max absolute difference between per-run rho estimates must be small (< 0.05) for both dG37 and CpG. This licenses the cross-premise-set replication claim. Note: this claim does NOT recompute the rhos — it asserts that two prior claims (hla_a_dg37_vs_tpm and hla_a_cpg_density_vs_tpm) observed the same pattern across two run premises with disjoint probe selection rules. The depends_on edge to the two child claims is the load-bearing structural commitment.
inference.failure_mode: If any per-run pair differed by > 0.05, the cross-run robustness claim would dissolve and the underlying associations would be premise-set-specific rather than universal.

conclusion.assertion: Two independent provenance runs with disjoint allele inclusion rules — exp24 (n=69 across 6 loci) and exp26 (n=22,259 total, n=19 HLA-A subset with TPM) — produce indistinguishable Spearman rho point estimates for HLA-A whole-allele dG37 vs TPM (-0.71 vs -0.711) and CpG density vs TPM (+0.70 vs +0.702). The HLA-A expression-biophysics signal is robust to premise-set variation.

scope.scale_note: Cross-run synthesis at the HLA-A level. Cross-locus replication (HLA-B, HLA-C) is queued as a sibling claim.

confidence.note: Replication-style claim — confidence is in the structural agreement of two independent runs, not a single p-value.

external_assumptions.statement: exp24 (n=69, all 6 loci) and exp26 (n=22,259, HLA-A subset n=19) operate on overlapping but distinct allele inclusion rules; the agreement is therefore informative about cross-premise-set robustness, not double-counting.
